segments.text: Here's a sorted array of numbers. We want to find the value twenty-three.
We set two pointers — one at each end of the array.
We check the middle element. It's sixteen — that's less than twenty-three.
So we know twenty-three must be in the right half. We move the left pointer up.
The new middle is thirty-eight — that's too big.
So twenty-three must be in the left half. We move the right pointer down.
We check the middle again — it's twenty-three!
Binary search found the target in just three steps, instead of checking all nine elements one by one.
